result.summary: Skyia is a React/Vite frontend with a PHP/MySQL backend designed for standard shared hosting. It features an immersive sci-fi terminal interface, AI model integration, and a progression system. The project is technically solid but requires maintenance updates for security, performance, and new features.
result.suitability: Mistral can assist with implementing the requested features and audits based on the provided documentation and codebase structure. However, direct file writes are not allowed, and all changes must be reviewed by Codex before application.

proposed_actions.title: Implement Homepage Novedades Section
proposed_actions.why: Enhances user engagement by showcasing updates and news.

proposed_actions.title: Dual-Mode Report Persistence in MySQL
proposed_actions.why: Ensures data reliability and backup for user reports.

proposed_actions.title: Model Latency Top 10 Checks
proposed_actions.why: Improves user experience by monitoring and optimizing AI model response times.

proposed_actions.title: Unavailable Model Diagnosis/Removal Policy
proposed_actions.why: Ensures system robustness by handling unavailable AI models gracefully.

proposed_actions.title: Security Audit Items
proposed_actions.why: Maintains system security and compliance with best practices.

proposed_actions.title: Hosting MCP Diagnosis
proposed_actions.why: Ensures smooth operation and compatibility with the hosting environment.

files_to_create.reason: Adds a new section to the homepage for displaying updates and news.
Implements dual-mode report persistence for local and cloud storage.

files_to_update.instructions: Integrate the NovedadesSection component into the homepage layout.
files_to_update.reason: Adds the NovedadesSection to the main application layout.

files_to_update.instructions: Add a new table for storing reports in MySQL.
files_to_update.patch:
--- a/database/schema.sql
+++ b/database/schema.sql
@@ -10,3 +10,10 @@ CREATE TABLE users (
   created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
 );
 
+CREATE TABLE reports (
+  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
+  user_id INT NOT NULL,
+  report_data JSON NOT NULL,
+  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
+  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
+);
files_to_update.reason: Adds a new table for storing user reports in the database.

risks: Changes to the database schema require careful migration to avoid data loss.
New components need thorough testing to ensure they integrate well with the existing UI.
Security considerations must be reviewed for any new data persistence methods.
questions_for_user: Should the NovedadesSection be styled to match the existing sci-fi terminal theme?
Are there specific security requirements for the dual-mode report persistence?
What is the preferred method for diagnosing and removing unavailable AI models?
